{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Logic change in conditional passkeys handling introduces a potential issue with user state validation during reauthentication flows.",
  "circularTests": {

  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Logic Error",
      "file": "services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/browser/UsernamePasswordForm.java",
      "line": 160,
      "title": "Null pointer risk in isConditionalPasskeysEnabled",
      "description": "The method isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user) checks user != null at the end, but is called in line 114 with context.getUser() which can be null. The challenge() method at line 137 also calls this with potentially null user. This creates an inconsistency: callers may pass null, but the method is designed to handle null. However, the real issue is that in line 114, the logic changed from checking 'user is not already selected' to 'user != null', inverting the condition during reauthentication. During reauthentication, context.getUser() is NOT null, so conditional passkeys will be enabled and webauthnAuth.fillContextForm() will execute even when it shouldn't for the initial form display.",
      "suggestion": "Verify the intent: if conditional passkeys should be skipped during reauthentication form display (initial authenticate call), the condition should remain checking if user is null OR reauthenticating. Add explicit reauthentication context check rather than inverting the user null check."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Control Flow",
      "file": "services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/browser/UsernameForm.java",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "Incomplete conditional logic for reauthentication skip",
      "description": "The condition now checks 'context.getUser() != null && !isConditionalPasskeysEnabled()' to skip the form. This means the form will be shown if conditional passkeys ARE enabled, even during reauthentication. However, the comment says 'We can skip the form when user is re-authenticating', implying the form should be skipped. The added condition inverts the expected behavior: when passkeys are conditionally enabled, the form will be displayed instead of skipped, contradicting the stated intent.",
      "suggestion": "Clarify the logic: if the form should always be skipped during reauthentication (when user is set), the condition should be 'if (context.getUser() != null)' without the passkeys check. If passkeys should force form display, update the comment to reflect this."
    }
  ]
}
